competition: 48th International Mathematical Olympiad Vietnam 2007 Shortlisted Problems with Solutions
gold_final_answer: Partition the indices into n+1 consecutive blocks of length n. For block v (0 ≤ v ≤ n), the first n−v entries are 0 and the last v entries are 1. Equivalently, writing any index as u + v n with 1 ≤ u ≤ n and 0 ≤ v ≤ n, the sequence is given by a_{u+vn} = 0 if u + v ≤ n and a_{u+vn} = 1 if u + v ≥ n + 1.
topics_flat: Discrete Mathematics > Combinatorics > Counting two ways
Algebra > Algebraic Expressions > Sequences and Series > Sums and products
response_text:
Let  
\[
S_i=a_{i+1}+a_{i+2}+\cdots+a_{i+n}\qquad (0\le i\le n^2-n).
\]
Then condition (b) is exactly
\[
S_i<S_{i+n}\qquad (0\le i\le n^2-n).
\]
Since each \(S_i\) is an integer, this means
\[
S_{i+n}\ge S_i+1.
\]

Now fix a residue class mod \(n\). For \(r=1,2,\dots,n\), consider
\[
S_{r-1},\ S_{r-1+n},\ S_{r-1+2n},\ \dots,\ S_{r-1+(n-1)n}.
\]
This is a chain of \(n\) integers, each at most \(n\), and each at least 1 more than the previous. Hence it must be
\[
1,2,\dots,n.
\]
So for every \(r\), we have \(S_{r-1}=1\).

Thus every block of \(n\) consecutive terms has sum \(1\):
\[
a_{i+1}+\cdots+a_{i+n}=1\qquad (0\le i\le n^2-n).
\]

Subtract consecutive equal sums:
\[
(a_{i+2}+\cdots+a_{i+n+1})-(a_{i+1}+\cdots+a_{i+n})=0,
\]
so
\[
a_{i+n+1}=a_{i+1}\qquad (0\le i\le n^2-n-1).
\]
Hence the sequence is periodic with period \(n\):
\[
a_{k+n}=a_k \quad \text{for all } k.
\]
Since every block of \(n\) consecutive terms has sum \(1\), the first period must contain exactly one \(1\) and \(n-1\) zeros.

Therefore the sequences are precisely the ones of the form
\[
(\underbrace{0,\dots,0}_{n\text{ terms with exactly one }1})\ \text{repeated } n+1 \text{ times},
\]
i.e. choose one position among \(1,2,\dots,n\) to be \(1\), set all other entries in that period to \(0\), and repeat this \(n\)-term pattern \(n+1\) times.

So there are exactly \(n\) such sequences.

Final answer: The sequences are exactly the periodic sequences with period \(n\) whose period contains exactly one \(1\) and \(n-1\) zeros; equivalently, choose one of the \(n\) positions in a block of length \(n\) to be \(1\), and repeat that block \(n+1\) times.

grade.predicted: The sequences are exactly the periodic sequences with period \(n\) whose period contains exactly one \(1\) and \(n-1\) zeros; equivalently, choose one of the \(n\) positions in a block of length \(n\) to be \(1\), and repeat that block \(n+1\) times.
